page: Hetzner Cloud cax21 is an ARM64-based virtual server instance powered by the QEMU hypervisor. It features 4 shared vCPUs from the Ampere Altra processor family, specifically utilizing the Neoverse-N1 microarchitecture running at 2.0 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 8.0 GB of system memory, providing approximately 1.95 GB of RAM per core, and is bundled with 80 GB of local SSD storage. No dedicated GPU hardware is present on this instance, and it does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Performance benchmarks indicate a moderate capability profile. The instance achieves average tier results in stress-ng single-core (1805.19) and multi-core (7235.85) CPU tests, alongside average performance in single-threaded gzip compression (24.04 GB/sec) and PassMark database operations (2796.83). However, memory and cryptographic performance represent notable tradeoffs. The PassMark memory score (1087.43) and CPU encryption test (646.59) fall into the poor category, while Geekbench multi-core (3310.0) and static web serving throughput (4.75 GB/sec) are categorized as weak.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the cax21 offers a highly accessible entry point for ARM64 development and lightweight hosting due to its shared resource model and low-overhead hardware class. It is best suited for general-purpose workloads that do not require sustained high-performance CPU or memory bandwidth, such as basic web serving, lightweight database operations, software compilation testing, and development environments.
description: Hetzner Cloud cax21 is an ARM64 virtual server featuring 4 shared vCPUs powered by an Ampere Altra Neoverse-N1 processor running at 2.0 GHz. Equipped with 8.0 GB of RAM and 80 GB of local SSD storage, this instance operates on the QEMU hypervisor without a dedicated GPU. Benchmark data shows average performance in stress-ng CPU tests and PassMark database operations, though it exhibits weaker results in memory bandwidth, cryptography, and Geekbench multi-core tests. Given its shared CPU allocation and budget-friendly hardware profile, the cax21 represents a cost-efficient option for lightweight workloads. It is highly suitable for basic web serving, development environments, software compilation, and low-intensity database tasks that do not demand sustained, high-performance compute resources.
og_description: Hetzner Cloud cax21 is an ARM64 virtual server featuring 4 shared Ampere Altra vCPUs, 8.0 GB of RAM, and 80 GB of local SSD storage, offering a budget-friendly option for lightweight workloads.

tagline: A budget-friendly ARM64 virtual server with shared Ampere Altra cores and local SSD storage for lightweight development and web hosting.

Includes 8.0 GB of RAM and 80 GB of local SSD storage
Delivers average performance in database operations and basic CPU stress tests
Exhibits lower performance in memory bandwidth and cryptographic tasks
